$comment: The suggested-plugins catalog. Funke fetches this file from the default branch and shows it in
Settings -> Plugins -> Browse. Adding an entry is a pull request, and merging one is a review:
an installable plugin runs as a normal program with the user's full rights, so please read the
Every entry pins `url` AND `sha256` (of the .zip). The launcher refuses any download whose hash
does not match, so a release asset cannot be swapped out after review — bumping a plugin's
version therefore means a new PR with a new hash. The archive must contain the plugin's folder
(`<id>/plugin.json` + its entry point), and the manifest's `id` must equal the `id` here.

plugins.name: Template
plugins.description: Transforms your query text (UPPERCASE, lowercase, reversed) — the plugin authoring starting point, in Rust
plugins.author: klappstuhlpy

plugins.name: Template (Python)
plugins.description: The same demo in ~70 lines of dependency-free Python — the starting point for Python plugins (needs Python on PATH)
plugins.author: klappstuhlpy
